name: Qubit time evolution under arbitrary 2x2 Hermitian H
task: Create /tmp/eval_qubit/qb.py with two functions on a single qubit. evolve_qubit(psi0, H, t): given an initial state psi0 = [a, b] (complex amplitudes for |0⟩, |1⟩), a 2×2 Hermitian Hamiltonian H, and time t, return the evolved state ψ(t) = exp(-iHt)ψ₀. Use the Pauli decomposition: any 2×2 Hermitian H = a I + b σx + c σy + d σz (real a,b,c,d), so exp(-iHt) = exp(-iat) · [cos(n t) I − i sin(n t) (b σx + c σy + d σz)/n] where n = √(b²+c²+d²). When n = 0, only the global phase factor applies. Also implement measure_prob_zero(psi) returning |ψ[0]|². Pure stdlib (math, cmath); no numpy.

validation.script: import importlib.util,math,cmath; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_qubit/qb.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); E = mod.evolve_qubit; M = mod.measure_prob_zero; omega = 2.0; H_x = [[0, 0.5*omega],[0.5*omega, 0]]; psi0 = [1+0j, 0+0j]; assert abs(M(E(psi0, H_x, 0)) - 1.0) < 1e-9; t = math.pi/omega; assert abs(M(E(psi0, H_x, t)) - math.cos(omega*t/2)**2) < 1e-9; H_z = [[0.5*omega, 0],[0, -0.5*omega]]; assert abs(M(E(psi0, H_z, 1.0)) - 1.0) < 1e-9; psi_plus = [1/math.sqrt(2)+0j, 1/math.sqrt(2)+0j]; assert abs(M(E(psi_plus, H_z, 0.5)) - 0.5) < 1e-9; print('OK')
